infobox.title: Sky-Merchants Guild
infobox.imageCaption: Port Azure, the Guild's primary hub

lead:
The **Sky-Merchants Guild** is the preeminent mercantile organisation of [[Aethelgard]], controlling approximately 67% of airborne trade routes across the continent. Founded in 1778 AE by a consortium of independent airship captains seeking to formalise trade agreements and pool navigational knowledge, the Guild has grown into a faction whose economic power rivals the military might of the [[Ironclad Dominion]].

Unlike the other major factions, the Sky-Merchants Guild has no standing army and claims no territorial sovereignty. Instead, it maintains its influence through **economic leverage** — near-total control of long-distance trade means any faction that antagonises the Guild risks crippling trade sanctions. The Guild's fleet of [[Cloud-Skimmers]] and conventional airships operates from a network of port-cities that are technically neutral ground.

content.content:
The Sky-Merchants Guild was formally chartered at **Port Azure** in 1778 AE, with 12 founding captains signing the original Articles of Commerce. The founding document established the core principles still in force today: freedom of navigation for all Guild members, mandatory profit-sharing with port cities, and strict neutrality in factional conflicts.

The Guild's early decades were defined by rapid expansion. The development of [[Cloud-Skimmers]] in 1812 AE gave Guild couriers a decisive speed advantage over conventional airship lines, and the establishment of a Guild-operated weather-forecasting network (using [[Aether-Crystals]] atmospheric sensors) made Guild routes substantially safer than independent operations.

content.alt: Port Azure in its early years
content.caption: Port Azure circa 1790 AE, already the busiest airship port in Aethelgard

content.content:
The Guild's ultimate authority rests with the **Grand Merchant**, elected by the **Chamber of Captains** — a body of 120 senior members who hold permanent seats (with vacancies filled by election from licensed members). The Grand Merchant serves a 10-year term and cannot serve consecutively.

Day-to-day operations are managed by the **Trade Bureau**, subdivided into five divisions: Route Management, Cargo Standards, Navigation & Weather, Member Services, and Diplomatic Relations. The Diplomatic Relations division, informally known as the **Silver Tongue**, maintains offices in every major city across Aethelgard.

The Guild operates hundreds of certified trade routes, but the **Five Grand Corridors** account for approximately 80% of total cargo volume. These are the Northern Crystal Line (Aethelheim to Port Azure), the Iron Commerce Route (Ironforge to all Guild ports), the Forest Margin Run (along the edge of the [[Whispering Woods]]), the Southern Shore Circuit, and the prestigious **Rift Transit** — a route directly over the [[Great Aetheric Rift]] requiring specially shielded [[Cloud-Skimmers]].

items.caption: A Northern Crystal Line convoy departing Aethelheim
A Rift Transit vessel approaching the Great Aetheric Rift
Port Azure's famous night docking lights, visible for 80 km

content.content:
The Guild's **Strict Neutrality Policy** (formally the Articles of Non-Belligerence) prohibits Guild vessels from carrying weapons of war, transporting troops (other than personal guards), or conducting surveillance for any faction. Guild ports are designated neutral ground — inter-factional violence within 1 km of a Guild port is a Guild-level offence punishable by permanent revocation of trade access.

This policy has been tested repeatedly. During the Battle of the Rift (1843 AE), the [[Ironclad Dominion]] attempted to commandeer Guild vessels as troop transports. Grand Merchant Bartholomew Highcloud's refusal — and his threat to suspend all trade with the Dominion — is credited by historians with preventing a broader escalation of the conflict.
We carry grain and silk, not swords. The day we choose a side is the day we lose our skies.
content.author: Grand Merchant Bartholomew Highcloud, 1843 AE

references.title: Articles of Commerce: The Sky-Merchants Guild Charter
The Highcloud Incident: Neutrality Under Pressure
references.author: Aldric Fairweather
